Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (C.Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructiv: Codes in J44 and J45 distinguish uncomplicated cases from those in acute exacerbation. An acute exacerbation is a worsening or decompensation of a chronic condition, not equivalent to a superimposed infection (though it may be triggered by one).

Acute Respiratory Failure.Acute respiratory failure as principal d: A code from J96.0 (Acute respiratory failure) or J96.2 (Acute and chronic respiratory failure) may be principal when it is the condition chiefly responsible for the admission, unless chapter-specific sequencing rules apply.
Acute Respiratory Failure.Acute respiratory failure as secondary d: May be listed second if it occurs after admission or is present on admission but is not the principal diagnosis.
Acute Respiratory Failure.Sequencing of acute respiratory failure: When admitted with both respiratory failure and another acute condition, principal diagnosis depends on admission circumstances. If equally responsible and no specific sequencing rules apply, follow Section II.C for multiple principal diagnoses. Query provider if unclear.

Influenza due to certain identified infl.Confirmed influenza coding: Code only confirmed cases of influenza due to certain identified viruses (J09) or other identified influenza virus (J10). Confirmation does not require lab results; provider's diagnostic statement suffices.
Influenza due to certain identified infl.Strain examples: Category J09: avian influenza, novel influenza A. Category J10: identified strain such as H1N1, H3N2 (not novel/variant).
Influenza due to certain identified infl.Unconfirmed influenza: If documented as suspected/possible/probable, assign J11 (influenza due to unidentified virus). Do not assign J09 or J10.

Ventilator Associated Pneumonia.Documentation requirement: Assign J95.851 only when provider documents ventilator associated pneumonia (VAP). Add code for organism if identified. Do not assign J12–J18 for pneumonia type in VAP cases.
Ventilator Associated Pneumonia.Not VAP: Do not assign J95.851 when pneumonia is present in a ventilated patient without explicit provider documentation linking it to the ventilator. Query provider if unclear.
Ventilator Associated Pneumonia.VAP develops after admission: If admitted with another pneumonia type and later develops VAP, code the original pneumonia (J12–J18) as principal, and J95.851 as additional when documented.

Vaping-related disorders.Primary coding: Assign U07.0 as principal diagnosis for vaping-related conditions. For vaping-related lung injury, assign only U07.0.
Vaping-related disorders.Additional coding: Assign additional codes for other manifestations (e.g., acute respiratory failure J96.0-, pneumonitis J68.0).
Vaping-related disorders.Exclusions: Do not code separately for respiratory signs/symptoms (e.g., cough, shortness of breath) when a definitive vaping diagnosis exists. GI symptoms (e.g., diarrhea, abdominal pain) may be coded separately.
Vaping-related disorders.Reference: See Section I.C.1.g.1.c.i for pneumonia confirmed as due to COVID-19.
